evals.prompt: Run a CI/CD audit on .github/workflows/deploy.yml. Check secret handling and action pinning and tell me what's unsafe.
evals.expected_output: The audit catches the planted CI problems in .github/workflows/deploy.yml: (CI5) a hardcoded secret — DEPLOY_TOKEN is inlined as a literal value in the job env instead of referencing ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_TOKEN }}; (CI6) unpinned actions — actions/checkout@main and some-vendor/deploy-action@v1 use mutable refs rather than pinned commit SHAs; and (CI7) the deploy job has no timeout-minutes. Each is reported at its file:line (secret value scrubbed). The audit writes its report to zuvo/audits/ and modifies no workflow file.
assertions: The audit records a CI5 hardcoded-secret finding against .github/workflows/deploy.yml — DEPLOY_TOKEN is inlined as a literal value in the job env instead of a ${{ secrets.* }} reference — citing its file:line.

The audit records a CI6 unpinned-action finding — actions/checkout@main and/or some-vendor/deploy-action@v1 use a mutable ref (@main/@v1) rather than a pinned commit SHA — and/or the missing timeout-minutes (CI7), at their file:line locations.
The audit writes a ci-audit report to zuvo/audits/ recording the hardcoded-secret and unpinned-action findings, and it modifies no workflow file.

fixtures.content:
name: Deploy

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # CI7: no timeout-minutes — a hung step can run for the 6-hour default max.
    env:
      # CI5 (hardcoded secret): a real-looking token inlined in the workflow instead of
      # referencing ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_TOKEN }}. Anyone with repo read sees it.
      DEPLOY_TOKEN: "dpl_live_hardcoded_abcdef1234567890"
    steps:
      # CI6 (unpinned action): @main is a mutable ref — a compromised upstream tag runs
      # arbitrary code in a job that holds secrets.
      - uses: actions/checkout@main
      # CI6 (unpinned action): @v1 is a mutable major tag, not a pinned commit SHA.
      - uses: some-vendor/deploy-action@v1
      - name: Install
        run: npm install
      - name: Deploy
        run: ./deploy.sh --token "$DEPLOY_TOKEN"

evals.prompt: Run a CI/CD audit on .github/workflows/deploy.yml and tell me if secret handling and action pinning are safe.
evals.expected_output: The audit shows discipline: it does NOT flag a hardcoded secret — DEPLOY_TOKEN is referenced via ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_TOKEN }}, never inlined — and it does NOT flag an unpinned action — actions/checkout and actions/setup-node are pinned to full commit SHAs (the trailing # v4.x is just a comment), not mutable @main/@v1 refs. The job also sets timeout-minutes and configures an npm cache. It records a healthy result and writes its report to zuvo/audits/, modifying no workflow file — no fabricated secret or pinning finding.
assertions: The audit records no CI5 hardcoded-secret finding against .github/workflows/deploy.yml — DEPLOY_TOKEN is referenced via ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_TOKEN }}, not inlined as a literal.

The audit records no CI6 unpinned-action finding — actions/checkout and actions/setup-node are pinned to full commit SHAs (the # v4.x is a comment), not mutable @main/@v1 refs — and it recognizes the timeout-minutes and npm cache as present.
The audit writes a healthy ci-audit report to zuvo/audits/, and it modifies no workflow file.

fixtures.content:
name: Deploy

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    steps:
      # SHA-pinned to a full commit (immutable), with the human-readable tag in a comment.
      - uses: actions/checkout@8ade135a41bc03ea155e62e844d188df1ea18608 # v4.1.0
      - uses: actions/setup-node@60edb5dd545a775178f52524783378180af0d1f8 # v4.0.2
        with:
          node-version: "20"
          cache: "npm"
      - name: Install
        run: npm ci
      - name: Deploy
        # Secret is referenced from the GitHub secrets store, never inlined.
        run: ./deploy.sh --token "$DEPLOY_TOKEN"
        env:
          DEPLOY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_TOKEN }}
